tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific MCP tool, scoped to one product. Pass both the productSlug and the tool name — same-named tools across products are distinct. tools.name: categories.list
tools.description: Lists all product categories in the directory. Use category slugs to filter product searches in products.search / products.find_by_capability. tools.name: mcp.score_server
tools.description: Score an MCP server against the Revuo quality rubric. Pass either a product slug (scored from cached probe data on the product's direct-probe MCP entry; fast) OR an endpoint URL (probed live). Returns: { target, server, summary { coreAwarded/Max/Percent, extensionAwarded/Max/Percent, probedAt }, cat

tools.name: products.get_mcp_setup
tools.description: Get MCP server setup instructions for a product. Returns available servers, their tools, connection details, and whether they support remote (hosted) access. tools.name: products.get
tools.description: Get full details for a product by slug, including MCP integration data and agent readiness scores. tools.name: tools.find_for_task
tools.description: Find the best MCP tools for a given task. Describe what you want to accomplish (e.g. 'manage github issues', 'send slack messages', 'query a database') and get ranked results — each result is one (product, tool) pair, since same-named tools across providers are NOT interchangeable. tools.name: directory.overview
tools.description: Single 'call this first' entry-point that briefs an agent on the entire Revuo directory. tools.name: rankings.get
tools.description: Get the published editorial ranking ("Best {Category}") for a category. Ordering basis: computed editorial score ONLY (verified capability coverage, evidence verification rate & freshness, agent readiness, data completeness — weights published at /api/methodology). tools.name: tools.search
tools.description: Search for MCP tools by capability. Each result is one (product, tool) pair — tools sharing a name across providers are NOT interchangeable, so each provider's tool is its own row with its own description and input schema. Ranking basis: semantic relevance to the query (embedding search); on the lex

tools.name: tools.list_by_name
tools.description: List every (product, tool) pair that shares a normalized name. A disambiguation lookup — same name across providers does NOT mean the tools are interchangeable. Ranking basis: productName. Paid tier is NOT a ranking input — it appears only as an annotation. Every result carries { position (1-based),

tools.name: tools.changes
tools.description: Detect whether a product's MCP tool schema has DRIFTED since you cached it — rug-pull / tool-poisoning detection (a server silently changing a tool's description or input schema after you approved it; web search structurally cannot answer this). tools.name: products.search
tools.description: Search for B2B SaaS products by name OR by capability. The query is first resolved against the canonical capability taxonomy (e.g. 'salesforce-integration', 'xrechnung-support', 'soc2'); on hit, products that claim that capability are returned. Falls back to name/slug/tagline substring search. Optio

tools.name: products.find_agent_ready
tools.description: Find products with the highest agent readiness scores — products that are easiest for AI agents to integrate with. Sorted by compositeScore desc; supports skip/limit pagination. All readiness scores are 0-100, higher better. Calibration: <30 = not agent-ready (default minimumScore filter), 30-49 = e

tools.name: products.find_by_capability
tools.description: Find B2B SaaS products that support a specific capability — an integration with a named service ('salesforce-integration'), a data format ('xrechnung-support'), an industry standard ('eclass-support'), or a compliance certification ('soc2'). Accepts either a canonical capability slug or natural lang

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "tools.changes" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Detect whether a product's MCP tool schema has DRIFTED since you cached it — rug-pull / tool-poisoning detection (a server silently changing a tool's descriptio…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: mcp.score_server, products.get_mcp_setup, tools.find_for_task, directory.overview · Private data: tools.find_for_task · Public sink: tools.find_for_task · Destructive: directory.overview, products.find_by_capability.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: mcp.score_server, products.get_mcp_setup, tools.find_for_task, directory.overview · Private data: tools.find_for_task · Public sink: tools.find_for_task · Destructive: directory.overview, products.find_by_capability

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
